teaching_language: English (study track of the master's degree in Ingegneria Gestionale)

academic_requirements.summary: Applicants need a bachelor's degree or equivalent with a minimum three-year undergraduate background in Management Engineering, Industrial Engineering, or related subjects, together with proven skills in Engineering. Country-based entry-title and GPA requirements also apply.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: Bachelor's degree or equivalent; minimum three-year undergraduate degree in Management Engineering, Industrial Engineering, or related subjects.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: Applicants must show proven skills in Engineering.
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: Country-based specific entry-title and GPA requirements apply on the official University of Padua entry-title page. No universal programme-specific GPA threshold is separately published on the course page.

accepted_proofs: IELTS Academic or General Training: minimum score 6.0.
Cambridge English B2 First: minimum score 173.
Trinity College London Integrated Skills in English ISE II.
Oxford Test of English: minimum score 126.
GATEHOUSE ESOL International Classic B2: GA Level 1 Certificate.
Pearson Test of English General: PTE General 3.
Pearson English International Certificate: Level 3 Upper Intermediate.
Pearson Test of English Academic: minimum score 65.
exemption_rules: Applicants are exempt if their previous education leading to the entry qualification was completed entirely in English.
Applicants are exempt if they are native English speakers from one of the countries listed by the University of Padua.

application_deadline_eu.notes: The third call is only for EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy.

application_deadline_non_eu.call_three: Not applicable for non-EU applicants residing outside Italy.
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: The third call is restricted to EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy.

required_documents.notes: Upload a colour scanned PDF; an English or Italian translation must be provided if needed.

required_documents.document_name: Academic qualification or provisional enrolment certificate
required_documents.notes: If the final qualification has not yet been awarded, an enrolment or provisional certificate is accepted.
EU citizens and non-EU citizens residing in Italy may use an ID card; other applicants normally provide a passport.
Required only for non-EU applicants residing in Italy.

required_documents.document_name: English language certificate or other accepted proof
required_documents.notes: Required unless the applicant qualifies for an exemption under the University language rules.

required_documents.document_name: Original and legalised bachelor's degree with Italian or English translation
required_documents.notes: Required after admission to complete enrolment.

required_documents.document_name: Original and legalised Transcript of Records with Italian or English translation
required_documents.notes: The transcript must be issued by the competent academic authority and show passed exams and grades.

required_documents.document_name: Universitaly pre-enrolment and study visa D - enrolment
required_documents.notes: Non-EU citizens residing outside Italy must pre-enrol through Universitaly no later than 15 July and then obtain a study visa.

required_documents.document_name: Residence permit or residence-permit application receipt
required_documents.notes: Required for non-EU applicants at enrolment if they are in Italy.

entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: The official pages reviewed do not state a programme-specific written test or interview; admission is based on degree background, subject preparation, and the formal application review.

source_quotes.quote: Access Open access with admission requirements
A minimum three-year undergraduate degree (or equivalent) in Management Engineering, Industrial Engineering, or related subjects with proven skills in Engineering.
The programme is a study track – held completely in English – of the Master’s degree programme in Ingegneria Gestionale
31st August for open access degree programmes.
Call Three: 2 June 2026 – 2 August 2026 (only for EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy)
